title: sonchus arvensis subsp. humilis
intro: Sonchus arvensis subsp. humilis, also known as the upright sow thistle or prickly sow thistle, is a subspecies of the annual or biennial plant Sonchus arvensis. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is commonly found in fields and along roadsides. The medicinal properties of Sonchus arvensis subsp. humilis include its potential to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, which are derived from its leaves and roots. The plant's leaves can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function.
